diagrams.title: Population and Sample Relationship
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchical relationship from population to sample to observations
diagrams.purpose: Understand the fundamental concepts of statistical inference and the flow of generalization from sample to population

diagrams.title: Sampling Distribution Formation
diagrams.description: Illustrates how sampling distribution is formed by taking multiple samples
elements: Population → Sample 1 (x̄₁) → Sample 2 (x̄₂) → ... → Sample k (x̄ₖ) → Sampling Distribution of x̄
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how statistics vary across different samples and form a distribution

diagrams.title: Confidence Interval Concept
diagrams.description: Shows the interpretation of confidence interval as capture rate over repeated sampling
elements: Multiple confidence intervals from different samples
Some capture true μ, some miss
95% confidence means 95 out of 100 intervals capture the parameter
diagrams.purpose: Understand that confidence level refers to long-run capture rate, not probability for any single interval
